Empire Algo Picks, Friday February 6th 2026 (1u) St Johns +2 (-120) Let's break this one down, because this is a perfect spot for St. John's at home in front of a fully packed Madison Square Garden. If you're not from NewYork, it's hard to fully appreciate how different this environment is. UConn has been playing 11 straight Big East games in much smaller arenas with much smaller crowds, and now they walk into 20,0o0 people at MSG on a Friday night. St. John's is used to this stage, and it shows in their results, as they are 10-2 at home this season. There's also a very clear coaching edge here... St. John's is 12-2 against the spread in their last 14 games vs UConn. It's obvious Rick Pitino knows how to attack Dan Hurley's teams, ge beat UConn twice last year, including a 14-point win at MSG, and this matchup has been circled on his calendar for a long time. St. John's is also playing their best basketball of the season right now, riding an eight-game winning streak, with several of those wins coming in dominant fashion, including margins of 12, 22, 24, 27, and 14 points. They don't see many ranked opponents outside of UConn tonight and then again in a few weeks on the road, and with UConn sitting at 12-0 in conference and St. John's right behind at 10-1, this game is essentially a must-win if St. John's wants a real shot at the Big East title. The betting market only makes this spot even better, this line opened at St. John's +1.5 and has barely moved, despite over 80% of the bets and the money coming in on UConn both on the spread and the moneyline. That's exactly what you want to see when you're fading the public, because it sets up a massive liability spot for the books if St. John's comes through athome. On the court, this St. John's team is built to give UConn problems, they're led by a strong high-low scoring duo in Ejiofor and Hopkins, both seniors, who are combining for 29 points and 13 rebounds per game and have been a matchup issue for just about everyone. UConn is not great at defending the paint, and that's exactly where St. John's wants to attack. On top of that, this team is deep, with six players averaging over 8.5 points per game and four averaging 11+, and the entire rotation is made up of experienced upperclassmen who have been in big games like this before. This veteran team in a huge home environment with a coach who consistently out-schemes this opponent, and with the conference race on the line. Pitino has had this one circled all year, and I expect a strong, disciplined, and aggressive game plan on both ends of the floor.
